title: Capstone: Build the Self-Debugging Sandbox
concept: Assembling a sandboxed environment that runs agent-generated Python code, returns runtime errors, and prompts correction; 'Next up: Computer & Web Use Agents.'.

exercises.prompt: What is the first step in Test-Driven Development?
exercises.explain: The first step in Test-Driven Development (TDD) is to write the test first, before the implementation code.

cards.title: Building the Self-Debugging Sandbox
cards.body: In this lesson, we will create a special environment where our AI agents can run their code safely, find errors, and learn to fix them.

cards.title: Debugging
cards.body: Debugging is the process of finding and fixing problems in code. A self-debugging system helps to identify these issues automatically.

cards.title: Sandbox as a Playground
cards.body: Think of a sandbox as a safe playground for experiments. Our Self-Debugging Sandbox allows AI to try out code without breaking anything important.

cards.title: Running Code in the Sandbox
cards.body: An AI agent writes a Python code that has an error. In the sandbox, it tries to run the code and discovers it didn’t work. It can then see the error and fix it right away.

cards.title: Myth: Debugging is Hard
cards.body: Many believe debugging is hard, but with automatic tools, it can be made easy! The Self-Debugging Sandbox simplifies this process.

cards.title: Visualizing the Sandbox
cards.body: Imagine two environments: one where AI can safely test and fix code, and another where mistakes can break things. We want the first one!

left.text: Running code without safety could cause crashes.

right.text: A safe place where errors can be fixed easily.

exercises.prompt: What does the Self-Debugging Sandbox do?

options.text: Runs code and finds errors automatically
Removes all errors from code immediately
Decides what code to write for the user

exercises.explain: The Self-Debugging Sandbox runs code and finds errors automatically, helping users understand and fix mistakes.

exercises.prompt: The Self-Debugging Sandbox can only run error-free code. True or False?
exercises.explain: This statement is false. The Self-Debugging Sandbox is designed to run code with errors and help the user correct them.

exercises.prompt: In the Self-Debugging Sandbox, the AI agent can run its code and find __________.
exercises.sentence: In the Self-Debugging Sandbox, the AI agent can run its code and find errors.
exercises.explain: The blank 'errors' refers to the issues that the AI agent can detect and correct in the sandbox.

exercises.prompt: Which of the following is an example of using the Self-Debugging Sandbox?

options.text: Writing code and hoping it works
Running code, seeing an error, and fixing it
Ignoring the output from running code

exercises.explain: The correct answer is running code, seeing an error, and fixing it—this shows how the sandbox helps with debugging.

exercises.prompt: What is the purpose of creating a Self-Debugging Sandbox?

options.text: To safely run code and detect errors
To only write code without checking
To waste time on unnecessary debugging

exercises.explain: The purpose of the Self-Debugging Sandbox is to safely run code and detect errors, helping programmers fix issues effectively. Next up: Computer & Web Use Agents.
